MacMobility
An interactive explorer of open urban-mobility data — traffic, transit, collisions, and cycling — city by city.
Choose a city

Hamilton, ON

10 datasets · signals, transit, collisions, cycling & more
Explore →
More cities coming

Your city?

The platform is built to drop in any city's open data.
Coming soon
About the data. Every dataset shown here is published as open data by the City of Hamilton on its Open Data portal (open.hamilton.ca) and is used here under those open-access terms. MacMobility only visualizes this public data; it adds no proprietary information. Additional cities will be added as their open mobility data is incorporated, with each city's source acknowledged here in the same way.
Note on routing & estimates. Trip routes and travel times are illustrative visualizations generated from public routing services and the road network — they are not verified navigation or safe cycling routes.
MacMobility — created by Amir Hossein Karbasi, PhD Candidate in Transportation Engineering, McMaster University,
under the supervision of Dr. Hao Yang, Associate Professor, McMaster University.
🚲

Stop density terrain
Show density heightfield
A glowing relief rises where stops cluster — pan the map and the terrain shifts beneath it, peaks marking the densest transit coverage (downtown, the B-Line corridor).
Show route lines
Animated buses on routes
Route polylines
Filter stops by amenity
Walk between stops
Tap Measure walk, then click two stops to get the walking distance and time along the streets between them.

Trip Summary

Estimated time by mode
Pace along the route
⚠️ Visualization only — this is not a verified bike route. The path is generated on the driving road network and may use highways, one-way streets, or roads that are unavailable or unsafe for cyclists. Times are rough estimates that ignore hills, the escarpment, traffic signals, and road conditions. Always confirm a safe cycling route yourself before riding — for example with a cycling-specific map or local bike-route signage.

Total spaces
822
Rack locations

Covered
–
Secure
Find nearest rack — set your location
Ride animation
Pick a location above to find the nearest rack, or tap Ride for a first-person-style ride between two points.
Density heat field
Pulses red where racks cluster
Show real bike lanes underneath
Move the cursor over the map — the field follows it and beats faster & redder where bike parking is dense (downtown, McMaster). Turn on bike lanes to see how routes relate to real cycling infrastructure.

Bikeways Network

Hamilton's cycling infrastructure · City open data
–
km of bikeways
–
km existing
–
km planned/upgrade
6
Facility types
Facility type — click to toggle
Dim planned / upgrade segments
Solid lines are existing bike infrastructure; planned upgrades show dashed. This is the real cycling network — the dedicated lanes, trails, and signed routes the ride animations elsewhere can't guarantee.

Truck Route Network

632 km · 1,250 segments · City of Hamilton review
632
km of routes
1,250
Segments
108
Restricted
72
Part-time
Color by road class
Highlight
🚫 Restricted segments only
⏱ Part-time segments only
Animated trucks on network
Hamilton designates these roads for heavy-truck use. Restricted and part-time segments carry time-of-day or vehicle limits — the rest are full-time. Click any road for its name and rules.

HSR Ridership & Service

City of Hamilton open data · monthly ridership 2014–2025
Annual boardings collapsed during COVID — from 22.3M (2019) to 11.8M (2020) and a low of 10.0M (2021) — then climbed back toward pre-pandemic levels by 2024.
–

Hamilton Bike Share

Snapshot · 190 hubs
–
Bikes available
–
Free docks
190
Hubs
–
Empty hubs
Color hubs by
Legend
Pulse low / empty hubs
Show rebalancing flow
Bikes ride the road network from surplus hubs toward empty ones — a hint of where the rebalancing van is needed.
Trip planner
Tap Plan a trip, then pick a start and an end (snaps to nearby hubs). A bike rides the route and we estimate the time. For visualization only — not a safe or recommended cycling route.

Hamilton Traffic Network · Reach

Signals + ITS · City of Hamilton open data▾
Layers
70%
Capillaries appear as you zoom in — local streets aren't drawn in the tiles at city-wide zoom.
Travel mode
Live tracking — field follows cursor
Move the mouse to sweep the reach field across the city; click to pin it in place, click again to release. Bands estimate 5 / 10 / 15 min reach (straight-line × detour factor — not routed times).
Within reach
No origin yet — move the mouse over the map (or tap on mobile) to light up coverage around a point.

≤ 10 min–
